title: Understanding Conditional Formatting
text: Conditional formatting in Google Sheets allows you to automatically change the appearance of cells based on certain conditions. This can be useful for highlighting important data, such as inventory levels.

title: Locating the Conditional Formatting Option
text: To apply conditional formatting, you need to access the Format menu in Google Sheets. This menu contains various options to change the appearance of your data.

title: Selecting the Correct Range
text: Make sure to highlight the range of cells that contain the flour inventory data. This ensures that the conditional formatting is applied to the correct data set.

title: Setting the Condition
text: In the conditional formatting rules, you need to set a condition that identifies when the inventory falls below $$100$$ bags. This will trigger the formatting change.
